symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid respiratory deterioration over 12 hours following a mild viral cold

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute respiratory decompensation of SMA / respiratory failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A viral trigger precipitating rapid decline is the classic mechanism of secretion-retention crisis in SMA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paradoxical see-saw breathing with chest-wall recession and severe tachypnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Impending hypercapnic respiratory failure (neuromuscular pump failure)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paradoxical breathing indicates the intercostal pump has failed and the diaphragm is fatiguing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weak ineffective cough with retained wet upper-airway secretions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ineffective cough in SMA is the direct mechanism of life-threatening secretion retention.

symptoms.symptom_name: Total feeding refusal with weak suck and swallow

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bulbar weakness impairs swallow and airway protection, raising aspiration risk and marking overall decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exhaustion and floppiness with preserved alertness (eyes tracking)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved cognition with physical exhaustion is characteristic of SMA and signals the pump is failing while the brain is intact.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline hypoxia — SpO2 ~91% on a home oximeter (room air)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoxia from atelectasis/mucus plugging; patient-obtainable via a home device so admissible in telehealth, but unverified and a lagging indicator in neuromuscular (hypercapnic) failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known SMA type 1 with severe flaccid hypotonia and diaphragm-dependent breathing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute respiratory failure from secretion retention
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The underlying neuromuscular weakness is what converts a trivial viral illness into a respiratory emergency — the single most important context.

symptoms.symptom_name: Auscultation: widespread transmitted rhonchi and decreased air entry at the bases (early atelectasis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician auscultation; the AI Doctor infers secretion retention from the wet cough and gurgling, not from breath sounds it cannot hear.

symptoms.symptom_name: Objective observations: BP 85/50, HR 165, absent deep tendon reflexes, tongue fasciculations

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: SMA type 1 with acute cardiorespiratory stress
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician measurements/examination — tachycardia reflects distress; absent reflexes and tongue fasciculations confirm the underlying anterior-horn disease. Not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Capillary/arterial blood gas and chest X-ray (hospital)

investigation_details.expected_result: Hypercapnia (raised CO2) with atelectasis/consolidation on imaging
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of ventilatory failure is missed; needed to guide non-invasive ventilation in PICU.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms type 2 respiratory failure — a hospital investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute respiratory failure from secretion retention in SMA type 1
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Paradoxical breathing + ineffective cough + exhaustion on a background of severe neuromuscular weakness is the constellation of impending respiratory arrest in SMA.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Continuous pulse oximetry (and, at hospital, blood gas)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Monitor for desaturation en route; hospital blood gas quantifies hypercapnic failure.
Identify atelectasis/consolidation from mucus plugging once in hospital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient bloods / septic screen in the community
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do NOT delay escalation to arrange community investigations — this is a 000 emergency; work-up happens in PICU.
